audit_findings.title: Delayed Remediation of Critical Vulnerabilities from Penetration Testing
audit_findings.criteria: DORA Article 11 requires financial entities to implement appropriate ICT risk management practices including timely vulnerability remediation
audit_findings.condition: Analysis of vulnerability remediation timelines revealed that 3 of 12 critical vulnerabilities identified in September 2024 penetration testing were not fully remediated until December 2024, exceeding the 30-day remediation SLA for critical findings
audit_findings.cause: Insufficient resource allocation to security remediation activities and competing priorities with Q4 operational demands
audit_findings.effect: Extended exposure window increased risk of exploitation; potential DORA non-compliance if pattern continues
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement dedicated security remediation sprint capacity; establish vulnerability management escalation procedures tied to SLA breaches; enhance executive reporting on remediation status
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Security team capacity being enhanced with additional FTE approved for Q1 2025. Escalation procedures being implemented.

audit_findings.title: Incomplete ICT Third-Party Risk Management Framework
audit_findings.criteria: DORA Article 28 requires financial entities to manage ICT third-party risk through comprehensive contractual arrangements and oversight
audit_findings.condition: Review of critical ICT vendor contracts identified that 4 of 15 critical technology vendors lack DORA-compliant contractual provisions including exit strategies and audit rights
audit_findings.cause: Legacy contracts predating DORA requirements; insufficient legal resources to complete contract remediation program
audit_findings.effect: Non-compliance with DORA third-party risk management requirements; limited ability to exercise oversight rights with certain vendors
audit_findings.recommendation: Prioritize contract remediation for critical vendors; establish DORA-compliant contract templates for new engagements; enhance vendor management procedures
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Contract remediation program accelerated with external legal support engaged. Target completion Q3 2025.

audit_findings.title: Inconsistencies in Client Retention Metric Calculation
audit_findings.criteria: Exit data room materials should present accurate and consistently calculated key performance indicators
audit_findings.condition: Review of gross retention rate methodology revealed inconsistent treatment of client departures due to fund liquidations versus competitive losses, resulting in 1.3 percentage point variance between alternative calculation methods
audit_findings.cause: Lack of documented policy defining retention calculation methodology; historical variations in treatment
audit_findings.effect: Potential for buyer challenge during due diligence; risk of misrepresentation if methodology not disclosed
audit_findings.recommendation: Document and formalize retention rate calculation methodology; reconcile historical calculations; ensure clear disclosure of methodology in data room
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. CFO to lead methodology documentation exercise with completion by March 31, 2025.

audit_findings.title: Incomplete Supporting Documentation for Revenue Pipeline Metrics
audit_findings.criteria: Data room materials should be supported by verifiable underlying documentation
audit_findings.condition: Testing of Q4 2024 sales pipeline data identified 8 opportunities totaling €2.1M where probability assignments could not be supported by documented client engagement evidence
audit_findings.cause: CRM data quality issues; inconsistent opportunity qualification criteria application by sales team
audit_findings.effect: Pipeline metrics may overstate near-term revenue potential; impacts reliability of forward-looking information
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement mandatory opportunity qualification checklist; enhance CRM data validation controls; conduct periodic pipeline review with documented outcomes
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Sales operations implementing enhanced qualification process and CRM controls.

audit_findings.title: CFO Transition Documentation Gaps
audit_findings.criteria: Key management transitions should be supported by comprehensive knowledge transfer documentation
audit_findings.condition: Review of CFO transition activities identified incomplete knowledge transfer documentation, particularly regarding non-standard accounting treatments and historical regulatory correspondence
audit_findings.cause: Extended acting CFO period (24 months) resulted in institutional knowledge gaps; limited transition overlap period
audit_findings.effect: Risk of inconsistent accounting treatment application; potential gaps in regulatory matter awareness
audit_findings.recommendation: Complete comprehensive knowledge transfer sessions; document all non-standard accounting treatments; establish repository for regulatory correspondence
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Accelerated knowledge transfer sessions underway with former Acting CFO. Documentation project initiated.

audit_findings.title: Privileged Access Management Control Weaknesses
audit_findings.criteria: DORA Article 9 requires appropriate ICT access controls proportionate to risk
audit_findings.condition: Review of privileged access identified 12 accounts with administrative access that had not been recertified within the required 90-day cycle; 3 accounts belonged to terminated employees
audit_findings.cause: Manual recertification process with insufficient tracking; incomplete integration with HR termination workflow
audit_findings.effect: Unauthorized access risk; potential for insider threat exploitation; DORA non-compliance
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement automated privileged access recertification workflow; enhance HR-IT integration for timely access removal; deploy privileged access management solution
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Immediate access review completed and terminated accounts disabled. PAM solution evaluation underway.

audit_findings.title: Insufficient Security Awareness Training Completion Rates
audit_findings.criteria: DORA Article 13 requires staff training on ICT security awareness
audit_findings.condition: Analysis of security awareness training records revealed 78% completion rate against 95% target; Operations department showed only 65% completion
audit_findings.cause: Insufficient management enforcement; training not integrated into performance objectives; competing operational priorities
audit_findings.effect: Increased human error risk in security incidents; non-compliance with regulatory training requirements
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement mandatory training completion tied to performance reviews; deploy completion tracking dashboard for managers; consider shorter, more frequent micro-learning modules
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. HR implementing training completion as gating factor for annual reviews. Enhanced tracking being deployed.

control_assessments.test_procedure: Reviewed privileged access recertification records and tested sample of 25 privileged accounts for compliance with 90-day recertification cycle
control_assessments.test_evidence: 12 accounts overdue for recertification; 3 accounts belonged to terminated employees

control_assessments.test_procedure: Analyzed security awareness training completion rates across all departments
control_assessments.test_evidence: Overall completion rate 78% vs 95% target; Operations at 65%

control_assessments.test_procedure: Reviewed ICT vendor contracts for DORA compliance requirements; assessed vendor risk assessment documentation
control_assessments.test_evidence: 4 of 15 critical vendors lack DORA-compliant contractual provisions

control_assessments.test_evidence: All sampled NAVs had documented review and approval; no exceptions identified

control_assessments.test_evidence: Gross retention methodology inconsistently applied; pipeline probability assignments unsupported

control_assessments.test_procedure: Reviewed financial close process documentation and CFO transition knowledge transfer
control_assessments.test_evidence: Incomplete knowledge transfer documentation from Acting CFO

control_assessments.test_procedure: Tested sample of 25 client onboarding CDD files for completeness and risk assessment accuracy
control_assessments.test_evidence: All sampled files contained required documentation; risk assessments appropriately documented

control_assessments.test_procedure: Reviewed sanctions screening configuration and tested sample of 50 alerts for appropriate resolution

control_assessments.test_procedure: Reviewed BCP documentation and DR test results; assessed DORA alignment
control_assessments.test_evidence: BCP current and tested; recovery time objectives achieved in testing; DORA certification obtained

objectives.name: Regulatory Compliance Excellence
objectives.description: Maintain full compliance with all applicable regulations including DORA, AIFMD, UCITS, MiFID II, and GDPR across all jurisdictions

objectives.name: Operational Risk Management
objectives.description: Maintain robust operational risk management framework with residual risks within defined appetite

objectives.name: Revenue Growth and Client Acquisition
objectives.description: Achieve annual revenue growth targets through new client acquisition and wallet share expansion

objectives.name: Service Excellence and Client Retention
objectives.description: Deliver exceptional client service to achieve 94% gross retention rate

objectives.name: Financial Performance and Reporting
objectives.description: Deliver accurate financial reporting and achieve EBITDA margin targets

objectives.name: Successful Exit Execution
objectives.description: Execute planned exit transaction with validated data room and management representations

objectives.name: Technology and Innovation
objectives.description: Leverage technology including AI capabilities to enhance service delivery and competitive positioning

standards_regulations.name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)
standards_regulations.description: EU regulation on digital operational resilience for the financial sector, establishing requirements for ICT risk management, incident reporting, and third-party risk management
standards_regulations.applicability: All Waystone EMEA entities providing financial services

standards_regulations.name: Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD)
standards_regulations.description: EU directive establishing regulatory framework for alternative investment fund managers including authorization, operating conditions, and transparency requirements
standards_regulations.applicability: Waystone Management Company and Fund Administration services

standards_regulations.name: UCITS Directive
standards_regulations.description: EU directive establishing framework for collective investment undertakings in transferable securities
standards_regulations.applicability: Waystone UCITS fund administration and ManCo services

standards_regulations.name: Markets in Financial Instruments Directive II (MiFID II)
standards_regulations.description: EU legislative framework regulating firms providing services linked to financial instruments and venues where instruments are traded

standards_regulations.name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
standards_regulations.description: EU regulation on data protection and privacy for individuals within the EU and EEA
standards_regulations.applicability: All Waystone entities processing personal data of EU residents

standards_regulations.name: Anti-Money Laundering Directives (4th, 5th, 6th AMLD)
standards_regulations.description: EU directives establishing framework for preventing money laundering and terrorist financing
standards_regulations.applicability: All Waystone entities providing financial services

standards_regulations.name: Central Bank of Ireland Regulatory Framework
standards_regulations.description: CBI regulatory requirements for fund service providers including authorization, capital, and conduct requirements

standards_regulations.name: CSSF Regulatory Framework
standards_regulations.description: Luxembourg financial regulator requirements for fund service providers

standards_regulations.name: FCA Handbook
standards_regulations.description: UK Financial Conduct Authority regulatory handbook requirements

standards_regulations.name: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers
standards_regulations.description: International accounting standard for revenue recognition

requirements.name: ICT Risk Management Framework
requirements.description: Financial entities shall have in place an internal governance and control framework that ensures an effective and prudent management of ICT risk (DORA Art. 5)
requirements.compliance_evidence: ICT risk management policy, governance structure, risk register

requirements.name: ICT Third-Party Risk Management
requirements.description: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk including contractual arrangements with ICT third-party service providers (DORA Art. 28-30)
requirements.compliance_evidence: Vendor risk assessments, contracts, oversight documentation

requirements.name: Digital Operational Resilience Testing
requirements.description: Financial entities shall maintain a comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme (DORA Art. 24-27)
requirements.compliance_evidence: Testing programme, test results, remediation records

requirements.name: ICT-Related Incident Reporting
requirements.description: Financial entities shall classify ICT-related incidents and report major incidents to competent authorities (DORA Art. 17-23)
requirements.compliance_evidence: Incident classification policy, incident logs, regulatory reports

requirements.name: Customer Due Diligence
requirements.description: Obliged entities shall apply customer due diligence measures when establishing a business relationship (AMLD Art. 13)
requirements.compliance_evidence: CDD policy, customer files, verification records

requirements.name: Ongoing Monitoring
requirements.description: Obliged entities shall apply ongoing monitoring of the business relationship including transaction scrutiny (AMLD Art. 13(1)(d))
requirements.compliance_evidence: Monitoring procedures, transaction monitoring alerts, investigation records

requirements.name: Suspicious Activity Reporting
requirements.description: Obliged entities shall promptly report suspicious transactions to the financial intelligence unit (AMLD Art. 33)
requirements.compliance_evidence: SAR policy, SAR filing records, FIU correspondence

requirements.name: Data Protection by Design and Default
requirements.description: Controllers shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to implement data protection principles (GDPR Art. 25)
requirements.compliance_evidence: Privacy impact assessments, data protection controls documentation

requirements.name: Data Breach Notification
requirements.description: Controllers shall notify supervisory authority of personal data breach within 72 hours (GDPR Art. 33)
requirements.compliance_evidence: Breach notification procedures, incident logs, regulatory notifications

requirements.name: Five-Step Revenue Recognition Model
requirements.description: Apply five-step model: identify contract, identify performance obligations, determine transaction price, allocate price, recognize revenue when obligations satisfied
requirements.compliance_evidence: Revenue recognition policy, contract analysis workpapers

requirements.name: AIFM Authorization and Operating Conditions
requirements.description: AIFMs shall be authorized and comply with operating conditions including substance, governance, and risk management
requirements.compliance_evidence: Authorization documentation, governance records, substance evidence

requirements.name: UCITS Depositary Requirements
requirements.description: UCITS shall appoint a single depositary to safekeep assets and perform oversight functions
requirements.compliance_evidence: Depositary agreements, asset verification records, oversight documentation

evidence.name: Q4 2024 Vulnerability Scan Report
evidence.description: Quarterly vulnerability scan results showing identified vulnerabilities and remediation status

evidence.name: September 2024 Penetration Test Report
evidence.description: External penetration test report identifying critical and high vulnerabilities

evidence.name: Privileged Access Recertification Records Q4 2024
evidence.description: Evidence of privileged access reviews and recertification completion status

evidence.name: Security Awareness Training Completion Report
evidence.description: Training platform report showing completion rates by department

evidence.name: DORA Compliance Certification
evidence.description: Documentation of DORA compliance assessment and certification achieved Q3 2024

evidence.name: ICT Vendor Contract Register
evidence.description: Register of critical ICT vendors and contract DORA compliance status

evidence.name: Data Room Documentation Index
evidence.description: Complete index of data room materials with validation status

evidence.name: Client Retention Rate Calculation Workpapers
evidence.description: Supporting calculations for gross retention rate metric

evidence.name: Sales Pipeline CRM Extract
evidence.description: CRM data export showing pipeline opportunities and qualification status

evidence.name: CFO Knowledge Transfer Documentation
evidence.description: Knowledge transfer documentation from Acting CFO to new CFO

evidence.name: AML/KYC Policy and Procedures
evidence.description: Current AML/KYC policies, procedures, and customer due diligence requirements

evidence.name: Sample CDD Files
evidence.description: Sample customer due diligence files for audit testing

evidence.name: NAV Calculation Review Checklists
evidence.description: Sample NAV calculation checklists showing review and approval

evidence.name: Revenue Recognition Policy
evidence.description: Current revenue recognition policy aligned with IFRS 15

evidence.name: Business Continuity Plan
evidence.description: Current business continuity plan including DR procedures

requests.title: Vulnerability Remediation Tracking Documentation
requests.description: Provide complete vulnerability remediation tracking from September 2024 penetration test through current date including timeline analysis

requests.title: Privileged Access Listing and Recertification Records
requests.description: Provide complete listing of privileged accounts with most recent recertification dates and evidence
requests.notes: Data received; exceptions identified requiring follow-up

requests.title: ICT Vendor Contract Register and DORA Assessment
requests.description: Provide register of critical ICT vendors with DORA compliance assessment for each contract
requests.notes: Register received; contract review in progress

requests.title: Data Room Complete Documentation Index
requests.description: Provide complete index of all data room materials with preparation status and validation records

requests.title: Key Metric Calculation Methodology and Supporting Evidence
requests.description: Provide documented calculation methodology for all key operational and financial metrics including gross retention rate

requests.title: Sales Pipeline Data and Qualification Evidence
requests.description: Provide CRM extract of current pipeline with supporting qualification documentation for top 20 opportunities
requests.notes: Data received; documentation gaps for several opportunities

requests.title: CFO Transition Knowledge Transfer Documentation
requests.description: Provide all knowledge transfer documentation from Acting CFO transition
requests.notes: Initial documentation received; additional items requested

requests.title: AML/KYC Policies and Sample CDD Files
requests.description: Provide current AML/KYC policies and sample of 25 recent client onboarding CDD files

requests.title: Revenue Recognition Policy and Contract Samples
requests.description: Provide current revenue recognition policy and sample of 20 contracts for recognition testing

requests.title: NAV Calculation Procedures and Sample Checklists
requests.description: Provide NAV calculation procedures and sample of 30 completed NAV checklists from Q1-Q2 2025

assessment_methods.name: Control Walkthrough
assessment_methods.description: End-to-end walkthrough of control execution with process owner to understand design and implementation

assessment_methods.name: Sample-Based Transaction Testing
assessment_methods.description: Selection and testing of sample transactions to verify control operating effectiveness
assessment_methods.sample_size_guidance: 25-40 samples for annual controls; 20-25 for quarterly; 15-20 for monthly

assessment_methods.name: Reperformance
assessment_methods.description: Independent reperformance of control activities to verify accuracy

assessment_methods.name: Inquiry and Observation
assessment_methods.description: Interviews with control owners and direct observation of control execution

assessment_methods.name: Data Analytics Testing
assessment_methods.description: Use of data analytics to test entire population or identify anomalies for targeted testing

assessment_methods.name: Configuration Review
assessment_methods.description: Review of system configuration settings to verify automated control implementation

assessment_methods.name: Documentation Inspection
assessment_methods.description: Review of control documentation, policies, procedures, and evidence

assessment_methods.name: Third Party Report Review
assessment_methods.description: Review and evaluation of SOC reports, certification reports, and third-party assessments

assessment_methods.name: Penetration Testing Results Review
assessment_methods.description: Review and validation of penetration testing results and remediation evidence

assessment_methods.name: Risk Assessment Review
assessment_methods.description: Evaluation of risk assessment methodology, documentation, and conclusions
assessment_methods.sample_size_guidance: Full risk register review; detailed review of high-risk items
